mnemonics: P.O.S. & S.O.P. - Paint One Square & Shade Other Pairs (for grouping adjacent 1s to find simplified product-of-sums and sum-of-products expressions)
keyPoints: The Karnaugh Map (K-Map) is a visual method for simplifying Boolean algebra expressions.
Adjacent '1's on the map can be grouped together in powers of two (1,2,4,8) to eliminate variables.
The goal is to find the fewest and largest possible groups to cover all '1's for the most simplified expression.
commonMistakes: Grouping non-adjacent cells; remember adjacency includes top/bottom and left/right edges (the map is cyclic).
Forgetting that 'don't care' (X) conditions can be included in groups to make them larger and simplify the expression further, but they don't have to be covered.
